config.cloudflare_tunnel.info: Securely expose local services to the internet through Cloudflare's global network using cloudflared. Supports Docker and native binary modes.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.disabled_desc: Enable the toggle to manage Cloudflare Tunnels for secure remote access to your local services.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.token_hint: Store your Cloudflare connector token in the Secrets Vault with key 'cloudflared_token'.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.named_hint: Store your tunnel credentials.json content in the Secrets Vault with key 'cloudflared_credentials'.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.expose_single_h: Token and Quick tunnels use a single origin URL. Only one service can be the tunnel destination. Use Named Tunnel for multi-service routing via separate hostnames.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.expose_named_hi: Named tunnels support multiple ingress rules — both services can be exposed simultaneously. Configure separate hostnames in the Custom Ingress rules.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.loopback_label: Loopback HTTP port for cloudflared (avoids TLS)
config.cloudflare_tunnel.loopback_hint: Opens a plain HTTP port on 127.0.0.1 only. cloudflared connects to this instead of the HTTPS port — no certificate verification needed. The port is assigned automatically.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.general_desc: Enable the tunnel and configure basic settings.
config.cloudflare_tunnel.connection_desc: Configure tunnel connection and authentication settings.
